# Harness Self-Improvement

## Continuous Feedback Loop

When you discover something during work — a new domain term, a gotcha, a missing rule — update the centralized harness IMMEDIATELY. Do not defer to session end. The next session gets the benefit in real-time.

## Health Indicators

A healthy harness means:
- Future agents succeed on the first pass without rediscovery
- Domain terms are consistent across all log entries
- Critical lessons are loaded automatically at startup via SessionStart hook
- No noisy hooks triggering without actionable output

## When to Update

| Discovery | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| New Eneco domain term not in ubiquitous language | Update `ddd-ubiquitous-language.md` immediately |
| On-call pattern not captured in domain rules | Add rule to `.ai/harness/rules/domain/` |
| Startup context was missing | Update `ddd-project.md` or `anchor-context-startup.md` |
| Repeated investigation of the same thing | Add lesson to `lessons-learned.json` |
| Hook triggering without value | Reduce hook scope or remove |

## Session-End Reflection

At session end, invoke the `codebase-harness-reflect` skill to capture friction, lessons, and harness gaps discovered during the session. If the skill is not invoked, the session-end hook will write a health report to `.ai/harness-health-report.md`.

## Health Report

If `.ai/harness-health-report.md` exists at session start, address it before other work. It lists specific gaps detected by the previous session's health check.
